titleStmt.title: Letter from John J. Ham to the Under-Secretary, 24 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Joseph Ham

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from John Joseph Ham to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), Under-Secretary for Ireland. Ham states that he has enclosed letters of recommendation in support of his candidature for the vacant position of Clerk of Crown and Peace for County Westmeath. Sir Robert Chalmers was sent to Ireland as replacement under secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5615/10750

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
10365, HALSTON, MOYVORE, Co. WEST MEATH 24 vi. '16 Sir Referring to my application
of 19th I now enclose here-
with some letters of re-
commendation in connect-
ion therewith. Your obt Servant John J. Ham Under Secretary: The Castle, Dublin
